input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-04-22T00:48:56.424Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
Here's how to make your page theme-aware:

## XAML ‚Äî `AppThemeBinding`

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page follows the system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#AAAAAA}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

`AppThemeBinding` reacts automatically when the OS theme changes ‚Äî no event wiring needed.

## C# alternative ‚Äî `SetAppTheme`

For code-behind or fully programmatic UI, use the extension method on any `BindableObject`:

```csharp
// in your page constructor or setup method
BackgroundColor = Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF");
this.SetAppTheme(
    BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"),   // Light
    Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E")); // Dark

var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppTheme(
    Label.TextColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#000000"),
    Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));
```

## Key points

| Concern | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Follows `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` (`Light` / `Dark` / `Unspecified`) |
| **Live switching** | Both approaches update instantly when the user toggles the OS theme |
| **Resource dictionaries** | For reuse, define colors in `App.xaml` with `AppThemeBinding` and reference them via `{StaticResource}` |
| **Manual override** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark` to force a theme regardless of system setting |

For larger apps, prefer defining themed colors as resources in `App.xaml` so you have a single place to update:

```xml
<!-- App.xaml -->
<Color x:Key="PageBg">{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}</Color>

<!-- MainPage.xaml -->
<ContentPage BackgroundColor="{StaticResource PageBg}" />
```
